model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Arizona is the slight pick because its overall profile is more balanced and stronger on the defensive end, with top-three rankings in both KenPom and Torvik compared with Purdue’s more offense-heavy build. Purdue absolutely has a credible path by leveraging the nation’s best offense and winning the shot-making battle behind its veteran core, so this is far from a comfortable call. But with Arizona also carrying an elite offense, the better defensive baseline, and no current injury signal strong enough to fade them, the Wildcats are the narrower choice to reach the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced Arizona profile over Purdue’s offense-first edge
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona rates higher overall in both major predictive systems and owns the much better defensive baseline, which gives it the safer all-around profile in a high-end Elite 8 matchup. Purdue’s elite offense makes the upset path very real and keeps confidence modest.
evidence: Arizona ratings: KenPom No. 3 (AdjEM 37.56, AdjO 127.2 No. 7, AdjD 89.7), Torvik No. 3 (AdjOE 126.22 No. 10, AdjDE 91.13 No. 3), record 31-2.
Purdue ratings: KenPom No. 8 (AdjEM 30.5, AdjO 131.0 No. 2, AdjD 100.5), Torvik No. 8 (AdjOE 132.56 No. 1, AdjDE 100.23 No. 36), record 26-8.
Matchup shape: Purdue has the best offense in the field by the available ratings, but Arizona is elite offensively too while being dramatically better defensively.

Availability context checked via web/news search: Arizona’s Koa Peat had a prior lower-body injury but returned before the tournament; no fresh major absence surfaced for either team in the available searches.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.
